revision.text:
[[ja:祇園大塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun | image = [[File:祇園大塚山古墳 出土品.JPG|280px]]
burial goods (Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}) | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu|1=Kisarazu}}, Gion 535, etc. (Oki) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.39380555555555|N|139.95125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length: 100m | burial = Joint-plate stone sarcophagus | burial goods = Gold-copper {{ill|Helmet with brow plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with brow plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Satellite Tombs = | built = Mid to late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = None | designation1 = | notes = Mound no longer exists | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Gion Ōtsukayama
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=23|coordinates=37.72|lon_deg=139|lon_min=57|lon_sec=4.45|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (祇園大塚山古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu|1=Kisarazu}}, Gion. It was part of the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The mound has since been lost. == Overview == Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun was a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed on the left bank of the alluvial plains of the {{ill|Obitsu River|en|Obitsu River|qid=Q7074982|lt=Obitsu River|1=Obitsu River}} in central Chiba Prefecture. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=Meiji 24|1=1891}}, local residents excavated the tomb, recovering numerous burial goods, but the mound has since been flattened. {{Sfn|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The mound was oriented with its front-facing section pointing west-northwest. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments were found on the mound surface, along with large {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars. A surrounding moat, possibly shield-shaped, enclosed the mound. {{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} The burial facility, recorded during the 1891 excavation, consisted of a joint-plate stone sarcophagus made from six flat stones. burial goods included a mirror with Buddhist and animal motifs (画文帯四仏四獣鏡), a gold-copper {{ill|Helmet with brow plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with brow plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}, silver long earrings, silver decorative plates, gold-copper armor plates, silver armor plates, iron arrowheads, and swords. {{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} Notably, the gold-copper helmet and armor plates were highly ornamental and likely non-functional, representing rare examples of such items in Japan. {{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} The tomb is dated to the mid to late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the mid {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Within the {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is considered a chief's tomb along the Obitsu River system, succeeding the {{ill|Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|高柳銚子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}}. Its size and burial goods place it among the most significant tombs in Chiba Prefecture. {{Sfn|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} == Mound area == Based on the "Chiba Prefecture Kimitsu District Kiyokawa Village map," the mound's area are estimated as follows: {{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} * Mound length: approximately 100m * Rear circular section diameter: approximately 55m * Neck width: approximately 30m * Front section width: approximately 55m == burial goods == [[File:祇園大塚山古墳出土 金銅製眉庇付冑 (J-728).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Gold-copper {{ill|Helmet with brow plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with brow plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}}}}]] burial goods from the tomb include: {{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} * Mirror with Buddhist and animal motifs (画文帯四仏四獣鏡) * Gold-copper {{ill|Helmet with brow plate|ja|眉庇付冑|1=Helmet with Brow Plate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with brow plate|13=User:Immanuelle/Helmet with Brow Plate|qq=}} * Silver long earrings * Silver decorative plates * Gold-copper armor plates * Silver armor plates * Iron arrowheads * Swords The mirror is stored in the Imperial Household Agency's Archives, while the other items are preserved at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The Tokyo National Museum also holds fragments of haniwa map_dot_labeled as originating from Gion, which may be associated with this tomb. {{Sfn|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}{{Sfn|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} (Taitō, Tokyo) - Houses burial goods from Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun. == References == {{small|(References used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Hiroshi Ozawa|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun > Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Ancient Tombs|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Ancient Tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun (Ancient Tomb Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1996|chapter=Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|title={{ill|Topography of Chiba Prefecture|ja|日本歴史地名大系 千葉県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Topography of Chiba Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|year=2003|chapter=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|title=Archaeological Resources of Chiba Prefecture II|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster (Archaeological Resources)|2003}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster]] * Subsequently, other kofun such as the {{ill|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|祇園大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}}, with an estimated length of 100 meters, were built near the fourth row o * {{ill|Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|祇園大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} – Estimated length: 100 meters. ... 6 KB (802 words) - 01:04, 15 Janu ary {{Kofun navbar}} }} {{translated page|ja|祇園大塚山古墳|version=96036029|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:祇園大塚山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q116694244}} [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kisarazu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
